{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Munasashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 =
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = Unknown
|家祖 = {{ill|Isachi-no-Atai|ja|海上五十狭茅|lt=Isachi-no-Atai|lt_ja=伊狭知直}}
|種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}})
|出身地 =
|根拠地 = Munasashi Province (later {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}})
|人物 =
|支流 =
}}
'''Munasashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''munasashi no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''munasashi kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the region that later became {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} ('''[[#Territory|Munasashi Province]]''').

In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', there is a '''{{ill|Muzashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|无邪志国造}}''' entry before the Munasashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko entry; there is a theory that these two kuni no miyatsuko are the same and another theory that they are different.

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
*According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', '''{{ill|Isachi-no-Atai|ja|海上五十狭茅|lt=Isachi-no-Atai|lt_ja=伊狭知直}}''', son of '''{{ill|Etamohi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命}}''' — ancestor of the {{ill|Kihe no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|岐閉国造}} — was appointed the first Munasashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
*According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}, Otohikotake-no-Mikoto, 3rd-generation descendant of {{ill|Kamu-Ise-tsu-hiko|ja|伊勢津彦|lt=Kamu-Ise-tsu-hiko|lt_ja=神伊勢都彦}} — ancestor of the '''Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' — was appointed {{ill|Sagamu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|相武国造}}.
*The "Kokuzō Hongi" lacks a record of the appointment period, but the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' estimates the period of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (the 15th {{ill|emperor|ja|天皇}}).

=== Clan ===
Unknown ({{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}).

== Base ==
From the position that it is a different kuni no miyatsuko from the Muzashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko, there is a theory locating it at present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市|lt=Gyōda|lt_ja=行田市}} in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}<ref name="chizu">''[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Primitive and Ancient Part II]]''.</ref> (former {{ill|Saitama District|ja|埼玉郡}} of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}).

=== Territory ===
The territory of the Munasashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko was the region then called '''Munasashi Province'''. Munasashi Province corresponds to the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|令制国}} of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}<ref name="chizu"/>, but is said to have covered the area excluding Chichibu and Saitama (which were {{ill|Chichibu Province|ja|知々夫国}} ruled by the {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}}).

For the origin of the place name, see {{ill|Musashi Province#Province name "Musashi"|ja|武蔵国#「武蔵」の国名}}.

Munasashi Province, along with {{ill|Muzashi Province|ja|无邪志国}} ruled by the {{ill|Muzashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|无邪志国造}} and Chichibu Province ruled by the {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}}, became the {{ill|ritsuryō|ja|令制国}} {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. Initially, Musashi Province belonged to the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, but in {{ill|771|ja|771年}} it was transferred to the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|東海道}}.

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[竹内理三]]等|title=[[日本歴史地図]]　原始・古代編　下|year=1982|publisher=[[柏書房]]|isbn=|page=289|chapter=|ref=chizu}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko#List|ja|国造#一覧|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko#List|lt_ja=国造#一覧}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:むさしのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:武蔵国]]
[[Category:東京都の歴史]]
[[Category:埼玉県の歴史]]
[[Category:神奈川県の歴史]]
[[Category:出雲氏|*むさしのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:胸刺国造]]
